REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO CLEARLY IDENTIFY COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART. ON SEPTEMBER 9, 2021 THE COURT EXECUTED A FORM OF JUDGMENT SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL OF RECORD. THE FORM OF JUDGMENT PREPARED BY THE DEFENDANTS DID NOT INCLUDE A DETERMINATION THAT DEFENDANTS ARE THE PREVAILING PARTY OR THAT THE DEFENDANTS ARE TO RECOVER COSTS. AT LEAST ONE OF THESE FINDING ARE NECESSARY TO ALERT THE CLERKS TO APPLICABILITY OF CRC 3.1700(B)(4) FOR THE PURPOSES OF ENTRY OF COSTS ON THE JUDGMENT ENTERED IN FAVOR OF A PREVAILING PARTY WHOSE MEMORANDUM OF COSTS MATURED. THE CLERK'S DUTIES ARE PURELY MINISTERIAL AND ARE BASED ON STRICT COMPLIANCE WITH PROCEDURE AND THE COURT'S ORDERS. THE FORM OF JUDGMENT HAVING NOT GIVEN ANY INDICATION THAT THE COSTS HAVE BEEN AWARDED (EXPLICITLY) OR COULD BE AWARDED TO THE PREVAILING PARTY (CCP 1032), WAS INCOMPLETE. THEREFORE, THE JUDGMENT IS AMENDED NUNC PRO TUNC TO INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING LANGUAGE: "DEFENDANTS TO RECOVER COSTS PER MEMORANDUM OF COSTS." (FOR FUTURE REFERENCE AND GUIDANCE SEE FORM JUD-100 6(B)). DEFENDANTS TO PRESENT AN ORDER IN CONFORMITY WITH THIS RULING AND A FORM OF AMENDED JUDGMENT. DEFENDANTS THEREAFTER MAY APPLY FOR AN ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501).

MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:05 am and concluding at 10:22 am.

Plaintiff Rodrigo Lopez is present represented by Rya Nelson, Esq., placed under oath and testifies.
Defendant Jorge Ponce not appearing.
Defendant Jorge Martinez Romero aka George Martinez Romero not appearing.

Plaintiff presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

RODRIGO LOPEZ is examined by the Court.

Court Finds:
1. The First amended complaint filed November 03, 2020 is the operative pleading.
2. The Defendants were served by substuted service on November 03, 2020.
3. The Statment of Damages was served upon Defendants by personal service on April 26, 2022.
4. The Doe defendants were dismissed on November 09, 2023.
5. The Default was entered as to both Defendants on August 18, 2022.
6. The Plaintiff has met their burden of proof as supported by the Declaration and Testimony of Rodrigo Lopez.

The matter is submitted.
By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

The Curt confirms that the Judgment requested will resolve the entire matter.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Rodrigo Lopez, and against Defendants Jorge Ponce and Jorge "George" Martinez Romero, as follows:

1. Damages: $50,000.00
2. Prejudgment Interest: $0
3. Costs: $2,972.54
4. Attorney Fees: $0
5. Other: $109,600.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $162,572.54

Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment To Clearly Identify Costs
Off Calendar Nov-17-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Michael Bracmontes 415-835-6777 of Bracamontes & Vlasak, P.C. appearing via Zoom for plaintiff.
Peggy Chang 510-548-7474 of Beckman, Feller & Chang appearing via Zoom for defendants Atlas Property Group, Inc. Et Al.

Counsel for plaintiff contests tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO CLEARLY IDENTIFY COSTS

is GRANTED in part. On September 9, 2021 the Court executed a form of judgment submitted by Defendants' counsel of record. The form of judgment prepared by the defendants did not include a determination that defendants are the prevailing party or that the defendants are to recover costs. At least one of these finding are necessary to alert the clerks to applicability of CRC 3.1700(b)(4) for the purposes of entry of costs on the judgment entered in favor of a prevailing party whose Memorandum of Costs matured. The clerk's duties are purely ministerial and are based on strict compliance with procedure and the Court's orders.
The form of judgment having not given any indication that the costs have been awarded (explicitly) or could be awarded to the prevailing party (CCP 1032), was incomplete. Therefore, the judgment is amended nunc pro tunc to include the following language: "Defendants to recover costs per memorandum of costs." (For future reference and guidance see form JUD-100 6(b)). Defendants to present an order in conformity with this ruling and a form of amended judgment. Defendants thereafter may apply for an Abstract of Judgment.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's tentative ruling.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
